# Fibonacci / domino tiling — f(n) = f(n-1) + f(n-2).
# Press Analyze, then step through the timeline to watch the recursion unfold.
# Look at the visual builder editor for more details.

n = 5

# @viz
RECT_COLOR = (128, 26, 134)
CIRCLE_COLOR = (143, 227, 136)

panel = Panel(x=1, y=1)
counter_label = Label(x=1, y=3, width=3)
panel.add(Rect(width=n, height=2, color=RECT_COLOR), counter_label)
dominoes = []
dominoes_x = 0    
counter = 0
# @end


def fib(n: int) -> int:
    if n <= 0:
        print('Empty Board!')
        # @viz
        global counter
        counter +=1 
        counter_label.label=f'counted {counter}'
        # @end
        return 1 # One (empty) way to fill an empty board

    # Either remove 1 and compute the rest:
    # @viz
    global dominoes_x
    dominoes.append(Circle(height=2, x=dominoes_x, color=CIRCLE_COLOR))
    panel.add(dominoes[-1])
    dominoes_x+=1
    # @end
    res = fib(n-1)
    # @viz
    dominoes.pop().delete()
    dominoes_x-=1
    # @end
    # Or remove 2 (if possible) and compute the rest:
    if n >= 2:
        # @viz
        dominoes.append(Circle(width=2, x=dominoes_x, color=CIRCLE_COLOR))
        dominoes.append(Circle(width=2, x=dominoes_x, y=1, color=CIRCLE_COLOR))
        panel.add(dominoes[-1], dominoes[-2])
        dominoes_x+=2
        # @end
        res += fib(n-2)
        # @viz
        dominoes.pop().delete()
        dominoes.pop().delete()
        dominoes_x-=2
        # @end
        
    return res

result = fib(n)
print(result)
# @viz
counter_label.label = f'Fib({n})={result}'
# @end
